items.text: Analyse, pressure check, and label the oxygen and diluent cylinders
Install both cylinders on the unit and verify correct installation
Verify the millivolts from the oxygen sensors
Sensor 1 (mV)
Sensor 2 (mV)
Sensor 3 (mV)
Sensor 4 (mV)
Sensor 5 (mV)
Verify the youngest oxygen sensor is less than 7 months old and reading correctly, add or replace if necessary, and mark date
Secure moisture absorber in the inhale lung under the non-collapse tube
Install the sensor grid and solenoid grid and verify the cable routing is correct
Fire the solenoid for at least ten (10) seconds, or at least three (3) times, and verify external battery reads greater than 6.5 volts
Inspect the remaining time on the scrubbers, and if needed, refill with fresh scrubber
Install the scrubber canisters into the unit with the TOP marker on the exhale scrubber (rMS arrows point to the top)
Inspect the scrubber O-Rings, clean sealant surfaces, and grease if needed
Inspect the non-return mushroom valves in the breathing loop mouthpiece
Inspect the mouthpiece and O-rings on the breathing loop, clean sealant surfaces, and grease
Install the breathing loop onto the unit
Wrap the HUD cable three to four times clockwise around the loop
Slowly open the oxygen valve, check the cylinder pressure, and manually add gas to verify proper function of the oxygen manual addition valve
Turn on the Shearwater and set it to the high set point, then verify the solenoid fires at least three (3) times
Set the Shearwater set point to 0.19
Perform three (3) full oxygen flushes with the DSV in the mouth, and then close and slightly reopen the DSV, leaving a small gap
Turn on the rEvodream(s) and calibrate them
Calibrate the Shearwater
Verify the external battery reads greater than 6.5 volts
External battery (in V)
Inspect the batteries of the Shearwater, and verify voltage is greater than 1.25/3.30/3.50
Internal Battery (in V)
Close the oxygen valve and verify that pressure drops
Depressurize the oxygen line
Open the diluent valve
Breathe in any gas from the loop to verify that the ADV is functioning
Close the loop and manually add diluent to verify proper function of the manual addition valve
Inflate the loop until the overpressure valve activates, then close the loop and verify that there is no pressure drop
Completely inflate the wing, and verify that it holds its volume
Flush with diluent until ppO2 drops to 0.21 - 0.22
Close the diluent valve and verify that pressure stays constant
Breathe in any remaining gas from the loop to perform a negative test
Analyze and label the bailout gas
Verify the cylinder pressure is appropriate for the planned dive and ensure the gas is compatible with the team
Inspect all connections
Close the bailout cylinder valve and check the regulator mushroom valve by inhaling
Ensure the necessary gases are programmed into the Shearwater
If the negative test is OK, turn off Shearwater and rEvodreams
Inspect the harness, crotch strap, and gag strap for wear and tear, correct positioning, and proper attachment
Open the bailout cylinder valve and verify that the pressure is appropriate for the planned dive
Breathe from the bailout cylinder to verify proper function
Inspect all connections
Ensure the diluent cylinder valve is open and verify cylinder pressure
Breathe in any gas from the loop to verify the auto-diluent valve is functioning
Manually add diluent and verify the cylinder pressure
Partially inflate the wing
Partially inflate the dry suit (if applicable)
Ensure the oxygen cylinder valve is open and verify cylinder pressure
Turn on the Shearwater and set to the Low Set Point, then verify that the solenoid fires
Manually add oxygen and verify the pressure
Verify that the rEvodreams are on
Verify that the ppO2 is greater than 0.7
Verify that rEvodreams and Shearwater batteries are within the acceptable ranges
Pre-breathe the unit for minimum of five (5) minutes or until the RCT is greater than 0:45, and verify that ppO2 is acceptable
Verify the gases are entered in the Shearwater computer, and that the computer is in closed-circuit mode
Inspect any other equipment required for the dive, including maks, find, spares, cutter, lights, etc.
